lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'AMD Athlon XP processors were discontinued circa 2005-2006, replaced by Athlon 64 (Socket 939/754) and then AM2 platform. No longer manufactured or supported.', 'source': 'https://forums.tomshardware.com/threads/amd-official-end-of-life-dates.295975/'}
notes: Processor line using GHz-E model numbers (1500+ through 2000+)

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'Intel Pentium 4 Willamette (130nm) was the first Pentium 4 core, released November 2000. Shipped until 2002 when Northwood replaced it. Fully discontinued by Intel circa 2008 for the broader P4 line.', 'source': 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pentium_4'}
notes: Competing processor; 2.0GHz Willamette used as AMD benchmark target

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'Intel Pentium 4 Northwood (130nm) was a key P4 variant from 2002-2004, replaced by Prescott. The entire Pentium 4 line was discontinued by August 2008.', 'source': 'https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pentium_4'}
notes: 130nm process, larger cache; obsoleted AMD's 2000+ equivalency claims

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': "BAPCo SYSmark 2001 is listed on BAPCo's End of Life Products page. BAPCo retired the product; licenses terminated, results no longer accepted. Superseded by SYSmark 2002, 2004, and subsequent versions.", 'source': 'https://bapco.com/end-of-life-products/'}
notes: Comprised ~1/3 of AMD GHz-E score; AMD did not file results with Bapco

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'SPEC CPU2000 was officially retired in February 2007. Starting November 2006, all submissions required an accompanying CPU2006 result. After February 2007, no further results were accepted. Superseded by SPEC CPU2006, then CPU2017.', 'source': 'https://www.spec.org/cpu2000/retired/'}
notes: Recommended by Aberdeen as more rigorous alternative benchmark

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'end-of-life', 'notes': 'Microsoft Windows XP reached end of extended support on April 8, 2014. No security updates since then. Fully end-of-life.', 'source': 'General knowledge / Microsoft lifecycle policy'}
notes: Launched Oct 2001; application patches altered benchmark results

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'AMD True Performance Initiative (TPI) was a circa 2001-2002 AMD program to address perceived benchmarking bias in SYSmark 2001. It was a short-lived marketing/technical initiative tied to the Athlon XP era. AMD abandoned TPI when moving to Athlon 64 and model number ratings system.', 'source': 'https://www.realworldtech.com/sysmark-2001/'}
notes: Announced Oct 2001 as replacement for GHz-E; Aberdeen applauded but noted slow progress

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'WinBench 2000 was a benchmark from Ziff-Davis/BizTech. The project was abandoned; WinBench 2000 never officially released (only WinBench 99 shipped). The benchmark suite was discontinued with the broader ET/BizTech benchmark program ending in the early 2000s.', 'source': 'https://www.storagereview.com/review/storagereview-coms-testbed3'}
notes: Used DirectX 7.0 while systems ran DirectX 8.1; called obsolete by Aberdeen
